items.title: HUD sets FY 2026 Housing Choice Voucher funding levels and key application deadlines
items.summary: HUD’s PIH Notice 2026-12 allocates about $34.557 billion for Housing Assistance Payment (HAP) renewals, $600.622 million for Tenant Protection Vouchers, and $2.835935 billion for administrative fees. The notice also sets multiple HAP set-aside application deadlines, including June 26, 2026 (by 5pm local time) for several categories such as unforeseen circumstances and project-based vouchers.

items.title: SSA posts official June 2026 payment dates for SSI and early Social Security recipients
items.summary: SSA’s 2026–2027 payment schedule shows SSI benefits are paid on June 1, 2026. The same schedule shows Social Security for people who began receiving benefits before May 1997 is paid on June 3, 2026.

items.title: USDA reiterates Summer EBT planning requirements and deadlines for agencies
items.summary: USDA’s Summer EBT 2026 toolkit states implementing agencies had to submit an initial Program Operations and Management plan (iPOM) by Aug. 15, 2025 to draw administrative funds. The toolkit also says a final POM was due Feb. 15, 2026 and must be approved to draw Summer EBT food benefit funds.

items.title: USDA publishes 2026–2027 income eligibility guidelines for free/reduced-price school meals and Summer EBT
items.summary: USDA’s notice sets income eligibility guidelines effective July 1, 2026 through June 30, 2027 for programs like the National School Lunch Program, School Breakfast Program, and Summer EBT. USDA notes the figures for a family of four in the 48 contiguous states reflect a 2.6% increase over last year’s level.

items.title: IRS inflation adjustments set higher Earned Income Tax Credit maximums for tax year 2026
items.summary: IRS Revenue Procedure 2025-32 increases the maximum EITC to $664 for workers with no qualifying children, $4,427 with one child, $7,316 with two children, and $8,231 with three or more children. The same guidance raises the investment income limit to $12,200 for tax year 2026.
items.source: Valor Tax Relief (citing IRS Rev. Proc. 2025-32)

items.title: USDA issues guidance memo transmitting 2026–2027 WIC income eligibility guidelines
items.summary: USDA’s Food and Nutrition Service posted a guidance document titled “Publication of 2026-2027 WIC Income Eligibility Guidelines” dated April 29, 2026. The memo transmits the updated income eligibility guidelines that WIC state agencies use for determining participant eligibility for the 2026–2027 period.

items.title: CMS updates Medicaid.gov Federal Policy Guidance index with new June 2026 entries
items.summary: Medicaid.gov’s Federal Policy Guidance page shows new guidance entries dated June 1, 2026, following prior updates on May 20 and May 5, 2026. The index is CMS’s running list of sub-regulatory guidance states use to interpret and implement Medicaid and CHIP policy.

items.title: CMS flags telehealth billing change for RHCs and FQHCs effective Oct. 1, 2026
items.summary: CMS’s MLN Connects newsletter notes rural health clinics and federally qualified health centers must bill the individual CPT/HCPCS code for distant site telehealth services instead of HCPCS code G2025 starting Oct. 1, 2026. CMS highlights this as an upcoming operational change providers should prepare for in advance of the effective date.
